Section.name: UNCLASSIFIED
Section.Content: Prepared by: Nicolas Rollier Date: 30/11/2023 – 1.0 Cegeka reference: [Comments] CONFIDENTIAL Jenkins Managed Service - Service Description

Section.name: VERSION CONTROL
Section.Content: General Document Jenkins Managed Service – Service Description Owner of document Nicolas Rollier Date 05/12/2024 Current version 1.0 Version Control Table Version Date Remarks / change log Author 0.1 12/11/2024 Initial document Kevin Van Poppel 1.0 05/12/2024 Published version Nicolas Rollier Reviewers Version Distribution to – Name of person Function / Role 0.1 Jess Dupont GCL Open Systems 1.0 Kevin Van Poppel DA Open Systems Approval Version Approval date Approved by (name person + role/function) 0.1 12/11/2024 Jess Dupont - GCL Open Systems 1.0 05/12/2024 Nicolas Rollier - HC Product Management

Section.name: Definition
Section.Content: Jenkins is an open-source automation server used for continuous integration (CI) and continuous delivery (CD). It helps automate various stages of the software development lifecycle, including building, testing, and deploying applications.

Section.name: Service Description
Section.Content: The Jenkins Managed Service, can be quoted or ordered using the following product SKU's :

Section.name: Service summary
Section.Content: This document describes the services the Open Systems team provides for the Jenkins Managed Service offering.

Section.name: Standard Services
Section.Content: Generic description The Open Systems team is committed to ensuring the availability of Jenkins services within the shortest possible timeframe, adhering to SLA, RTO, and RPO targets, while considering the inherent limitations of the team. All activities described below are part of the management of the service and directly linked to the Jenkins - Namespace Management (recurring) - SKU, which is billed on a recurring monthly basis. We will install and configure the Jenkins infrastructure to ensure it is ready for end-user use. However, any configuration within the application itself remains the responsibility of the end user. Our primary objective is to provide technical application support, which includes regular updates and patch management. Major version upgrades, however, are excluded from the scope of standard support and can be carried out under a separate, billable Request for Change (RFC). For scheduled activities, predefined service windows will be utilized. During these periods, Jenkins may experience temporary unavailability. All planned interruptions will be communicated in advance through the built-in banner feature within Jenkins. Note: theese SLA's are not applicable for bugs in community supported tools (Jenkins). Environment Day Hours Test Every 1st Tuesday of the month 07:00 - 09:00 Production Every 3rd Tuesday of the month 07:00 - 09:00 Backup and recovery Each Jenkins instance is backed up using an Image-Level Backup solution in accordance with the “ Cegeka - O.Open Systems - Preferred 1 month replicated ” backup policy. Monitoring and event resolution Each Jenkins instance is monitored using standard Cegeka monitoring tools, which include: Instances covered under this service are subject to both reactive and proactive monitoring. This is based on various criteria and triggering events, which may result in incidents being assigned to the Open Systems team. Monitoring-related incidents are managed according to their priority and within the designated service windows. Security baseline compliancy As with any server provided by our team, each Jenkins instance will have the latest approved Cegeka security baseline applied during the initial setup. Updates to the security baseline will be implemented via a Request for Change (RFC) when necessary. Capacity Management Cegeka ensures operational capacity monitoring for storage and compute resources through monitoring events. When predefined standard thresholds are exceeded, monitoring incidents are triggered with the appropriate priority for further action. Lifecycle Management Both the operating system and the Jenkins service will be patched regularly in alignment with Open Systems standards. Patching will follow the established cycles used for all managed servers and adhere to the DTAP (Development, Testing, Acceptance, Production) process. For Jenkins, the latest stable version will always be maintained. Minor and major updates will be applied systematically, ensuring that outdated versions of Jenkins are never in use. User Management User management is conducted in compliance with the prevailing security baselines, which include the following measures: Every new users access needs to be approved by an administrator. Access and permissions will be granted based on the engineers resolver group. Jenkins environment Within the Jenkins instance, each Competence Center (or resolver group, if no Competence Center is defined) is provided with an isolated environment. A recommended setup is outlined in our Best Practice - Jenkins guidelines 1 . While adherence to these recommendations is not mandatory, deviations may limit the availability of optional services. Outside the designated Competence Center group, creating additional groups is not permitted, ensuring enforcement of the predefined setup. 1 the corresponding document is stored in the Confluence space of the Open Systems team and if needed can be requested through the DA. Liability As Jenkins is an open-source tool, we cannot guarantee any SLA, RTO, or RPO commitments if the root cause of faulty behavior is attributable to a bug within the Jenkins software.

Section.name: Optional Services
Section.Content: Service accounts Service accounts can be created for granting access to the Jenkins API. Although this type of request is rather exceptional are rarely applies, the creation of additional service accounts can/should be requested/raised by creating a support ticket, as there is no automation supporting it.

Section.name: Service Request
Section.Content: Requests related to this Service can be ordered and will result in the execution of that request by means of a Cegeka change order. Each request has a throughput time to deliver the request (after approval or pre-approval on the customer’s CAB). Service request id Service request Throughput time Not applicable Not applicable Not applicable

Section.name: Cegeka owned licenses and/or subscriptions
Section.Content: These licenses and/or subscriptions and their cost are included in this Service. It is Cegeka’s responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. Licenses and/or subscriptions used for Enabling Services are included in the Service and are not listed below. License Included in service Applies to not applicable since open source -- --

Section.name: Customer owned licenses and/or subscriptions
Section.Content: These licenses and/or subscriptions and their cost are not included in this Service. It is Customer responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. License Description/remarks not applicable since open source --

Section.name: RACI Matrix
Section.Content: Listed below are the activities related to the Service, the activities RACI related to enabling services are listed in the Enabling Services Document. Cegeka Customer Namespace deployment and configuration A, R I Add additional Customer / Agent Configuration A, R I RACI Role definition

Section.name: Service Window
Section.Content: Priorities P1 P2 P3 P4 Service Window 24x7 Office Hours Office Hours Office Hours Response time 1 hour N.a. N.a. N.a. Resolve Time < 4 hours < 8 hours < 24 hours <40 hours RFC type Standard Type 1 Type 2 Type 3 Type 4 Implementation time Pre-defined per standard change Need-by date Need-by date Need-by date Need-by date Impact analysis and financial proposal NA 5 working days 5 working days 5 working days 5 working days
